title: Voluntary Consumer AI Disclosure Pilot Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Voluntary Consumer AI Disclosure Pilot Act aims to establish guidelines for companies to disclose the use of AI technology in their products or services. It is a pilot program that may eventually become a mandatory requirement for companies to be transparent about their AI practices.

title: Protecting Families from AI Data Center Energy Costs Act
action: Forwarded by Subcommittee to Full Committee (Amended) by Voice Vote.
summary: This policy aims to protect families from costs associated with energy consumption by AI data centers. The Act seeks to regulate or provide relief from the financial burden of powering AI data centers.

title: Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Environment and Public Works.
summary: The Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026 aims to mitigate the environmental effects of artificial intelligence technology. The policy seeks to address the potential harm caused by AI to the environment.

title: Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026
action: Referred to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, and in addition to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: This policy document, the Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026, does not provide a description. However, based on the title alone, it likely addresses the potential environmental impacts of artificial intelligence.

title: To require certain artificial intelligence model developers to submit reports to the Secretary of Commerce, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.
summary: This document outlines a policy that requires certain AI model developers to submit reports to the US Secretary of Commerce. The reports aim to promote transparency and accountability among AI developers for other purposes.

title: Artificial Intelligence Data Center Moratorium Act
action: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committee on Foreign Affairs, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Artificial Intelligence Data Center Moratorium Act aims to introduce a temporary halt on the development of new artificial intelligence data centers. This moratorium likely seeks to assess or mitigate the potential impact of these centers on the environment and technology infrastructure.

title: To direct the Comptroller General of the United States to conduct a study on the impacts of artificial intelligence on the workforce in the United States, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on Education and Workforce.
summary: This bill directs the Comptroller General to study the effects of artificial intelligence on US workers, with the goal of understanding its impact on the national workforce. The study aims to provide insights for policymakers to address potential issues arising from AI implementation in the workforce.

title: Sectoral AI Governance Act of 2026
action: Referred to the Committee on the Judiciary, and in addition to the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Sectoral AI Governance Act of 2026 is a law that aims to regulate and govern the development and use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) across various sectors of the economy. It likely establishes guidelines and standards for the safe and responsible deployment of AI technologies, while promoting innovation and growth.

title: Algorithmic Accountability Act of 2025
action: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.
summary: The Algorithmic Accountability Act of 2025 is a proposed law that aims to regulate and hold accountable technology companies for their algorithmic decision-making processes. This act likely seeks to address concerns around fairness, transparency, and bias in AI systems used by industries such as finance, healthcare, and advertising.

title: Federal Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Act of 2026
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Federal Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Act of 2026 is a policy aimed at managing and minimizing risks associated with artificial intelligence. The policy seeks to regulate AI systems and mitigate potential negative consequences in various aspects of American society.

title: SBA Artificial Intelligence Utilization Act of 2026
action: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Small Business and Entrepreneurship.
summary: This policy document outlines the SBA Artificial Intelligence Utilization Act of 2026, which likely focuses on how the Small Business Administration uses or regulates artificial intelligence. The exact details of the policy are unknown without more information, but the title suggests it addresses AI implementation in SBA policies.

title: A bill to address the effects of artificial intelligence-enabled systems, including artificial intelligence chatbots, on older adults, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions.
summary: This bill aims to address the impact of artificial intelligence and chatbots on older adults, with multiple potential purposes to be identified. The bill seeks to address the specific effects of AI-enabled systems on older adults, but its full scope is unclear.

title: Unleashing AI Innovation in Financial Services Act
action: Placed on the Union Calendar, Calendar No. 619.
summary: The Unleashing AI Innovation in Financial Services Act is a policy aimed at promoting and supporting the use of artificial intelligence in the financial services sector. Its purpose is to create an environment where financial institutions can innovate and develop AI solutions to improve services and operations.

title: China AI Power Report Act
action: Ordered to be Reported (Amended) by the Yeas and Nays: 47 - 0.
summary: The China AI Power Report Act is a policy document that focuses on Chinese artificial intelligence (AI) technology and its potential to enhance the country's power and influence. However, without further information, its specific contents and objectives remain unclear.

title: Responsible Artificial Intelligence Defense Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Armed Services.
summary: The Responsible Artificial Intelligence Defense Act of 2026 is a policy that aims to regulate the use of artificial intelligence. The specific details and goals of the act are not available due to the lack of description.

title: READ AI Models Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The READ AI Models Act is a policy that governs the use or creation of artificial intelligence models. However, with no description available, more information cannot be provided about its specific details or goals.

title: Workforce for AI Trust Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Workforce for AI Trust Act aims to establish a framework or guidelines to maintain trust in artificial intelligence systems by regulating their use in the workforce. The specifics of the implementation are unknown as no description is available for the policy.

title: K–12 AI Literacy and Readiness Act of 2026
action: Referred to the House Committee on Education and Workforce.
summary: The K-12 AI Literacy and Readiness Act of 2026 aims to prepare students in grades K-12 for an era where artificial intelligence plays a significant role in various aspects of life. This act aims to ensure that students acquire essential knowledge and skills required for success in an AI-driven society.

title: Preventing AI Censorship Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
summary: The Preventing AI Censorship Act aims to prohibit the use of artificial intelligence to censor or suppress free speech. This act is intended to protect online freedom of expression by preventing AI systems from deleting, hiding, or modifying content based on subjective or arbitrary criteria.

title: Generative AI Terrorism Risk Assessment Act
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs.
summary: This policy document, the Generative AI Terrorism Risk Assessment Act, aims to assess and manage the risk of generative AI being used for terrorist purposes. The document likely outlines procedures and guidelines to mitigate potential threats associated with AI-generated content.

title: Department of Advanced Technology and Artificial Intelligence Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform.
summary: This policy is likely related to the governance and regulation of advanced technology and artificial intelligence in a specific department or government agency.

title: Fraudulent Artificial Intelligence Regulations (FAIR) Elections Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Rules and Administration.
summary: The Fraudulent Artificial Intelligence Regulations (FAIR) Elections Act of 2026 is a policy that aims to create regulations to prevent the use of fake or manipulated artificial intelligence in elections. It likely seeks to ensure the integrity of the electoral process by preventing potential AI-related election interference and maintaining voter trust.

title: A bill to prohibit certain uses of automated decision systems by employers, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions.
summary: This bill aims to restrict how employers use automated decision systems, such as AI that makes hiring or firing decisions without human input. The bill seeks to regulate these systems for "other purposes" not explicitly stated.

title: A bill to amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to impose an excise tax on systemically important AI activity, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Finance.
summary: This bill proposes to add an excise tax on certain AI activities that are deemed "systemically important". The new tax would be designed to regulate and monitor the impact of these AI activities.

title: Secure and Accountable Military AI Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Armed Services.
summary: The Secure and Accountable Military AI Act of 2026 aims to ensure the safe and responsible use of artificial intelligence in military operations. It seeks to establish guidelines and safeguards to prevent potential misuse of AI technology.

title: Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act is a policy aimed at safeguarding consumers from misleading or inaccurate artificial intelligence technologies. The act seeks to establish regulations and guidelines to prevent deceptive AI practices and promote transparency in AI applications.

title: Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act
action: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act is a policy document aimed at shielding consumers from false or misleading uses of Artificial Intelligence. Its primary goal is to safeguard consumers' rights and protect them from potential harm caused by deceptive AI practices.

title: Heat Emergency Assessment and Tracking using AI Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.
summary: This AI policy aims to develop and implement a system using Artificial Intelligence to assess and track heat emergencies, potentially improving early warning systems and emergency response. The Heat Emergency Assessment and Tracking using AI Act is likely a legislative measure to establish and enforce guidelines for this AI system.
